home.title: Aoi Admin runs the core platform and many product lines
home.description: For developers, independent product teams, and growing SaaS teams, Aoi Admin reuses account, access, organization, configuration, audit, plugin, media, version, and system management foundations.

preview.title: One shared base, many independent product lines
preview.description: The core platform owns common capabilities while each product line keeps its own public site, business console, domain model, and experience.

site.label: Site
site.title: Public platform story
site.description: Home, platform narrative, blog, legal pages, auth entry points, and localized content.

setup.label: Activate
setup.title: First-install setup
setup.description: Configure locale, storage, database, cache, first account, and site details from the backend schema.

admin.label: Core
admin.title: Shared capability console
admin.description: Manage IAM, System, probes, media, versions, plugins, audit, and design-system settings.

productLines.label: Lines
productLines.title: Independent product entries
productLines.description: Future product lines can extend their own sites, consoles, and domain modules while reusing the platform.

surfaces.title: Site, setup, core console, and product lines share one base
surfaces.description: The current React frontend carries the brand story, safe activation flow, and shared business console; product lines can extend independent experiences on the same foundation.

site.title: Public website
site.description: Shows Aoi Admin's platform value, capability boundary, use cases, content articles, and auth entry points.

setup.title: First-install setup
setup.description: A separate wizard handles pre-activation configuration, connection tests, recovery paths, and safe credential handling.

admin.title: Shared platform console
admin.description: /admin manages backend-exposed core platform capabilities instead of product-line-specific business screens.

productLines.title: Future product-line entries
productLines.description: Each product line can own its site, business console, and domain modules while reusing accounts, orgs, permissions, and quality tools.

capabilities.title: Build every shared product capability once
capabilities.description: The Aoi Admin console only connects real backend capabilities; features without APIs, permissions, persistence, and audit contracts remain explicit boundaries.

items.iam: Accounts, roles, permissions, sessions, API tokens, and MFA
items.tenant: Organizations, membership, invitations, and audit context
items.configuration: System settings, menus, dictionaries, parameters, and public settings
items.audit: Operation records, audit logs, login logs, and error logs
items.plugins: Remote plugin registration, capability declarations, health, and management views
items.media: Media library, storage helpers, and resumable upload workflows
items.versions: Version packages, API catalog, probes, and runtime status

rails.title: Product lines can be independent; infrastructure cannot fork
rails.description: Every entry shares Aoi React components, API client, auth state, i18n, Markdown rendering, quality tooling, and the Go static hosting flow.

i18n.title: Central i18n
i18n.description: Public site, admin, setup, forms, SEO, empty states, errors, and accessibility labels live in zh-CN and en resources.

api.title: Single API client
api.description: Auth, refresh retry, normalized errors, abort signals, and X-Locale mapping stay in one integration layer.

markdown.title: Verified Markdown
markdown.description: Blog front matter is parsed with gray-matter, validated with Zod, rendered with react-markdown, and sanitized by default.

quality.title: Unified quality gates
quality.description: Visible UI, routes, i18n, Markdown, builds, and browser viewport checks become the shared frontend baseline.

cta.title: Activate the shared base, then extend independent products
cta.description: Use setup to configure the core platform, enter /admin to manage shared capabilities, and extend future product lines without rebuilding foundations.

about.title: Aoi Admin is a full-stack product platform base
about.description: It is not a single admin template and not just a Go backend scaffold; it is a runnable, extensible base for hosting multiple business product lines.

principles.title: Stable core platform, independent product lines
principles.description: Shared capabilities are governed in the core platform, while business products keep independent routes, experiences, and domain models.

evidence.title: Backend contracts define the boundary
evidence.description: The public site can describe the direction, but production features must be backed by Go APIs, config, permissions, persistence, and audit behavior.

boundary.title: Do not rebuild the platform base
boundary.description: Product lines must not duplicate accounts, organizations, permissions, configuration, audit, API client, i18n, or design-system foundations.

cleanup.title: Clean up old positioning and entries
cleanup.description: Current docs and frontend resources describe the shared product base instead of a single admin or temporary frontend rebuild.

architecture.title: Core platform + multiple product lines
architecture.description: Aoi Admin hosts common capabilities in one runtime, while product lines extend their own user journeys and domain features on the same base.

public.title: Public website
public.description: Home, platform story, blog, legal, login, and signup pages carry the brand and content layer.

setup.title: Setup flow
setup.description: The first-install wizard stays outside the public and platform console shells, using setup schema and status APIs to activate safely.

admin.title: Core platform console
admin.description: /admin manages shared capabilities including IAM, System, plugins, media, versions, audit, and design-system settings.

productLines.title: Independent product lines
productLines.description: Future product lines can own their public pages, business consoles, business modules, and domain models.

shared.title: Shared foundations
shared.description: Aoi components, tokens, API client, auth, TanStack Query, Zustand, i18n, Markdown, tests, and build flow stay shared.

cta.title: The platform story is unified; production features still follow the backend
cta.description: The site and docs can explain the multi-product direction, while real management operations only connect backend-exposed contracts.

blog.title: Product platform notes and engineering logs
blog.description: Blog content uses local Markdown with bilingual front matter, build-time validation, and safe rendering for platform positioning, architecture boundaries, and engineering decisions.
blog.emptyDescription: New articles must keep localized Markdown metadata in sync.

legal.termsDescription: This page keeps the public route and localization structure ready while final platform service legal text is reviewed before launch.
legal.privacyDescription: This page documents the privacy structure that must be confirmed against production hosting, auth, storage, and audit behavior before launch.

scope.title: Scope
scope.description: These draft terms describe the Aoi Admin platform surfaces and must be replaced with approved legal copy before public release.

accounts.title: Accounts
accounts.description: Login and signup forms only target backend-supported authentication contracts; final account rules belong in the approved terms.

content.title: Content
content.description: Local Markdown articles and product pages are maintained with bilingual metadata and must not imply unsupported production capabilities.

changes.title: Changes
changes.description: Legal changes must update both zh-CN and en resources in the same commit and pass the i18n validation command.

collection.title: Data collection
collection.description: The React app should only collect information needed by backend-supported auth, setup, platform console, and content workflows.

storage.title: Browser storage
storage.description: Auth tokens use browser session storage, locale preference uses local storage, and setup secrets stay in page memory only.

integrations.title: Backend integrations
integrations.description: API requests send X-Locale through the shared client and rely on the Go backend for persistence, authorization, and audit.

launch.title: Launch review
launch.description: Final privacy copy must be reviewed against production hosting, analytics, storage, authentication, and product-line extension behavior before release.

footer.description: Aoi Admin is a shared product base for the public site, setup, auth entry points, platform console, and future product-line entries.

setup.title: Platform first-install setup
setup.description: Complete the required configuration before enabling the shared product base. Sensitive fields are only used in this wizard session and are not written to browser persistent storage.

language.title: Choose language
language.description: The language applies immediately to every following step, validation message, and help text.

errors.confirmRequired: Confirm the final setup action before running initialization.
errors.ownerRequired: Fill in the initial account and organization information first.
errors.passwordMismatch: The initial account passwords do not match.
errors.skipNeedsRun: A setup run is required before this step can be skipped.
errors.stepBlocked: Resolve the failed or running prerequisite step before opening this step.
errors.testFailed: Connection test failed. Update the configuration and try again.

messages.savedEnvManagedOverwritten: Configuration saved. Environment-managed placeholder fields were written to the config file for: {{paths}}.

test.restartRequired: Restart the backend before continuing this setup step.
test.stale: The form changed after this test. Run the connection test again before continuing.

confirm.title: I understand initialization will apply the current configuration
confirm.description: The backend may run migrations, seed system data, create the first account, and issue login tokens based on these values.

report.title: Initialization report
report.restartRequired: Restart the backend, then reload setup status.

security.title: Credential safety
security.description: Database, cache, storage credentials, and the initial account password only stay in page memory. Refreshing the page requires entering them again.

empty.title: No setup steps available
empty.description: The backend did not return a setup schema. Check the service state.

passwordConfirm.label: Confirm initial password
passwordConfirm.help: Used only for this page validation. It is not sent to the backend or written to browser persistent storage.

login.title: Log in to Aoi
login.description: Use the existing Go backend auth endpoint to enter the platform console.
login.captchaDescription: Enter the captcha returned by the Go backend before submitting the password again.
login.mfaDescription: Enter the one-time code from the authenticator app linked to this account.

signup.title: Create account
signup.description: The signup form only targets the backend-exposed signup contract.
signup.loadingDescription: The signup form opens after the backend public settings are loaded.
signup.verificationPendingDescription: A verification link has been sent. The account and organization will activate after the link is confirmed.

signupVerify.title: Verify email
signupVerify.description: Confirm the email verification token and open the platform console.
signupVerify.loadingDescription: The backend is activating the pending account and organization.
signupVerify.missingTokenDescription: Open the verification link from the notification message and try again.

passwordForgot.title: Recover password
passwordForgot.description: Enter the account email. If the account exists, the backend will send a reset link through the configured notification channel.
passwordForgot.emailSentIfExists: If the email exists, a reset link will be sent.
passwordForgot.successDescription: Check the configured inbox or use the debug token returned by a debug notification driver.

passwordReset.title: Reset password
passwordReset.description: Use a one-time reset token and set a new password. Existing sessions for the account are revoked by the backend after success.
passwordReset.successDescription: Log in again with the new password.

invitation.title: Accept organization invitation
invitation.description: Use the invitation token to create or complete an account for the invited organization.
invitation.successDescription: Log in with the invited account to open the platform console.
invitation.missingTokenDescription: Open the invitation link from the notification message and try again.

guard.loadingDescription: The platform console opens after the browser session is restored.

messages.identityFailed: Account details could not be refreshed.

dashboard.title: Platform workspace
dashboard.description: The platform workspace reads health, readiness, server metric history, API catalog, and version records to show the real backend state of the shared product base.
dashboard.signedOutDescription: Log in before accessing the platform console. This page does not fabricate backend data.
dashboard.capabilitiesDescription: IAM, System, probes, media, versions, and plugin queries only connect to real APIs already exposed by the backend.

states.permissionDescription: The current account does not have the permission required by this backend endpoint.

serverInfo.title: Server status
serverInfo.description: The current snapshot comes from /api/v1/system/server-info and summarizes CPU, RAM, disk, and runtime data.

gauges.label: Server resource usage
gauges.diskEmpty: The backend did not return disk metrics

apiCatalog.description: Route catalog derived from the Go router and IAM permission rules.
apiCatalog.title: API catalog

serverMetrics.title: Server monitoring
serverMetrics.description: Short-window history comes from /api/v1/system/server-metrics/history and refreshes automatically on the backend sampling interval.
serverMetrics.chartAria: Network receive and transmit rate history chart
serverMetrics.diskChartAria: Disk read and write rate history chart
serverMetrics.diskEmptyDescription: This platform has not returned disk IO counters yet, or the sampler has not produced disk data to display.
serverMetrics.emptyDescription: The sampler fills the short history window after startup; no samples are available right now.

trafficHijack.chartAria: Traffic hijack probe latency trend chart
trafficHijack.description: Shows healthy targets, abnormal targets, recent events, and short-window latency from active HTTP(S) probes.
trafficHijack.emptyDescription: After a target is created and probed, the dashboard will show real trend data.
trafficHijack.title: Traffic hijack monitoring

runtime.title: Go runtime
runtime.description: Backend process, platform, and refresh time summary.

process.title: Process and build
process.description: Go memory, GC, and build version summary.

iam.title: IAM overview
iam.description: Read the backend IAM organization, member, role, permission, session, and audit log contracts without exposing unsupported write workflows.

currentOrg.title: Current organization
currentOrg.description: Organization context is resolved from the authenticated access token and confirmed against /api/v1/orgs.

states.emptyOrgDescription: The organization list did not include the current token organization. IAM scoped requests still use the token org ID.
states.loadingDescription: Reading protected IAM endpoints for the current organization.
states.missingOrgDescription: The current session token does not include an orgId claim, so organization-scoped IAM endpoints cannot be called.
states.permissionDescription: The current account needs the matching IAM read permissions to view this panel.

probes.title: Service probes
probes.description: Read the public /health and /ready probe endpoints without adding bearer authorization or unsupported operations.

health.title: Health probe
health.description: Lightweight liveness response from the Go HTTP router.

ready.title: Readiness probe
ready.description: Database readiness check returned by the Go HTTP router.

states.emptyChecksDescription: The backend returned a readiness status without check details.
states.emptyHealthDescription: The backend response did not include health data.
states.emptyReadyDescription: The backend response did not include readiness data.
states.loadingDescription: Reading the current backend probe response.

apis.title: API catalog
apis.description: Review the HTTP API catalog registered by the backend through /api/v1/system/apis.
apis.empty: No API entries match the current filters.

filters.title: Filters
filters.description: These filters run on the current backend catalog response.

list.title: API entries
list.description: {{count}} of {{total}} endpoints match the current filters.

sync.permissionsConfirmDescription: This calls /api/v1/system/apis/permissions/sync to create missing permission codes from the current backend API permission declarations without overwriting existing permission names or descriptions.
sync.permissionsStorageUnavailable: The backend returned {{total}} permission codes, but permission storage is {{status}}, so this result was not persisted.
sync.routesConfirmDescription: This calls /api/v1/system/apis/sync to sync the current Go route registry into the API catalog and refresh this table from the backend result.
sync.routesMemoryOnly: {{total}} endpoints were generated from runtime routes. The backend has no API storage connected, so this result is memory-only.
sync.routesPersisted: {{total}} endpoints synced, {{created}} created, {{updated}} updated, {{stale}} marked stale.
sync.routesStorageUnavailable: {{total}} endpoints were generated from runtime routes, but API storage is unavailable, so this result was not persisted.

states.loadingDescription: Reading the protected API catalog endpoint.
states.permissionDescription: The current account needs the permission:read permission to view the API catalog.

menus.title: Menu catalog
menus.description: Review the backend-filtered admin menu groups returned by /api/v1/system/menus for the current account.
menus.empty: No menu entries match the current filters.

filters.title: Filters
filters.description: These filters run on the menu catalog already filtered by the backend for the current account.

list.title: Menu groups
list.description: {{count}} of {{total}} menu entries match the current filters.

states.loadingDescription: Reading the authenticated backend menu endpoint.
states.permissionDescription: The current account cannot read the menu catalog returned by the backend.

dictionaries.title: Dictionary catalog
dictionaries.description: Manage dictionaries and dictionary items through the backend-supported system dictionary contract.
dictionaries.empty: No dictionaries match the current filters.

filters.title: Filters
filters.description: These filters run on the current backend dictionary catalog response.

list.title: Dictionaries
list.description: {{count}} of {{total}} dictionaries match the current filters, with {{items}} items visible.

delete.dictionaryDescription: {{name}} and its {{count}} dictionary items will be deleted through the backend dictionary contract.

form.codeHelp: Use lowercase letters, numbers, dot, underscore, or hyphen. The first character must be a letter or number.
form.dictionaryDescription: Submit only the code, name, description, and status fields accepted by the backend.
form.itemDescription: Item writes stay scoped to {{name}} and use dictionary:update or dictionary:delete permissions.
form.name: Dictionary name

placeholders.description: Optional usage notes for this dictionary
placeholders.name: System status

messages.createdDescription: {{name}} is now available in the dictionary catalog.

states.permissionDescription: The current account needs the dictionary:read permission to view dictionaries.
states.writePermissionDescription: The current account needs the {{permission}} permission for this dictionary action.
states.storageUnavailableDescription: The backend reports that system_dictionaries is not persisted yet. Run the required migrations before using dictionary workflows.

validation.dictionaryCodeDescription: Dictionary code must start with a lowercase letter or number and may contain only lowercase letters, numbers, dot, underscore, or hyphen.
validation.dictionaryRequiredDescription: Dictionary name and status are required.
validation.itemRequiredDescription: Dictionary item label, value, and status are required.
validation.itemSortDescription: Dictionary item sort must be an integer.

system.title: System settings
system.description: Read and update backend-editable runtime configuration values returned by /api/v1/system/config.
system.persistWarningDescription: Changes are submitted only for backend-marked editable fields and are persisted through the runtime config manager.

group.inactiveDescription: This group is hidden by another configuration value in the backend snapshot.

editor.arrayHelp: Enter one value per line. Empty lines are ignored before submission.
editor.defaultHelp: This value is sent to the runtime config manager when it changes.
editor.description: Only editable fields in this group are submitted. Blank secret fields keep their current value.
editor.numberHelp: Use an integer accepted by the backend config manager.
editor.objectHelp: Object values are displayed as text; update them only when the backend marks the field editable.
editor.secretHelp: Leave blank to keep the current secret.
editor.title: Edit {{label}}

states.emptyDescription: The backend response did not include configuration sections.
states.groupEmptyDescription: This group has no settings visible under the current backend values.
states.loadingDescription: Reading the protected configuration snapshot endpoint.
states.permissionDescription: The current account needs the config:read permission to view system settings.

media.title: Media library
media.description: Read media categories and asset records from /api/v1/system/media, then use the backend upload and URL import endpoints for supported write workflows.

filters.description: These filters map to the backend media asset list endpoint.
filters.title: Filters

categories.createDescription: Create a category under {{parent}} through POST /api/v1/system/media/categories.
categories.deleteDescription: This calls the backend category delete endpoint for {{name}}. The backend rejects categories that still contain child categories or media assets.
categories.nameHelp: Names are trimmed by the backend and must be unique under the same parent.
categories.parentHelp: Root catalog stores the category at the top level.
categories.requiredDescription: Enter a category name before saving.
categories.sortHelp: Lower values appear earlier within the same parent.
categories.title: Categories

states.permissionDescription: The current account needs the media:read permission to view the media library.
states.storageUnavailableDescription: The backend reports that media metadata is not persisted yet. Run the required migrations before using media workflows.
states.uploadUnavailableDescription: The backend reports that uploads are unavailable. Confirm object storage configuration before using media write workflows.

a11y.fileInput: Choose media files for direct upload

messages.categoryCreatedDescription: {{name}} was created in the media category tree.
messages.categoryDeletedDescription: {{name}} was deleted from the media category tree.
messages.categoryUpdatedDescription: {{name}} was updated in the media category tree.
messages.deletedDescription: {{name}} was deleted from the media library.
messages.downloadedDescription: {{name}} was downloaded through the authenticated backend endpoint.

rename.description: Update the display name for {{name}} through PATCH /api/v1/system/media/assets/:assetId.
rename.help: The backend keeps the original filename; this only changes the catalog display name.
rename.requiredDescription: Enter a display name before saving.
rename.title: Rename media asset

delete.confirmDescription: This will delete {{name}} through the backend media delete endpoint. Storage-backed objects are removed with their catalog record.

write.description: New assets are written to {{category}} through backend media endpoints.

import.description: Create external media records from HTTP or HTTPS URLs.
import.help: Use one URL per line. Optional names use the Name|URL format.
import.label: URL list
import.title: URL import

write.title: Upload and import

upload.description: Select one or more local files. Current backend limit: {{limit}}.
upload.title: Direct upload

designSystem.title: Design system settings
designSystem.description: Inspect the active source theme package, preview controlled token drafts, and verify recipes, templates, assets, and contrast without adding production theme APIs.

contract.title: Source theme boundary
contract.description: Aoi themes are selected at build time from local source packages. No runtime CSS upload, HTML injection, publish, audit, or rollback API is exposed in v1.

controls.title: Controlled semantic tokens
controls.description: The editor previews the active package defaults and exports a strict draft. Permanent theme changes are made in the source package and regenerated before build.

coverage.title: Token coverage
coverage.description: The active package declares every global definition it owns, from palette and layout to focus, overlays, charts, syntax, and motion.

contrast.title: Contrast checks
contrast.description: Each color pair is checked against the WCAG AA normal text threshold of 4.5:1 before it is treated as ready for a source theme package.

accentColor.help: Secondary semantic accent used for small highlights and supporting controls.
accentColor.label: Accent color

mode.help: Preview how the semantic palette behaves in the selected interface mode.
mode.label: Mode

motionIntensity.help: Controls preview transition duration and documents the intended motion level.
motionIntensity.label: Motion

primaryColor.help: Main brand action color for buttons, active navigation, and emphasis.
primaryColor.label: Primary color

radiusScale.help: Controls preview corner radius for panels and controls.
radiusScale.label: Radius

shadowLevel.help: Controls preview elevation depth for modular surfaces.
shadowLevel.label: Shadow

spacingScale.help: Scales the preview module density without changing the global layout.
spacingScale.label: Spacing scale

surfaceColor.help: Base surface color used for cards and work panels.
surfaceColor.label: Surface color

textColor.help: Primary readable text color for surface content.
textColor.label: Text color

typographyScale.help: Scales preview body copy and supporting text.
typographyScale.label: Typography scale

import.description: Import and export a strict token draft for review. This does not persist production themes or bypass the source package workflow.
import.help: The JSON must match the current schema exactly. Unknown fields and non-hex color values are rejected.
import.title: Import and export

errors.fileRead: The selected file could not be read.
errors.invalidJson: The imported payload is not valid JSON.
errors.invalidSchema: The imported payload does not match the approved theme token schema.
errors.title: Import failed

messages.defaultsDescription: The editor has been reset to the approved default token draft.
messages.exportedDescription: The current draft has been exported as JSON.
messages.importedDescription: The imported token draft is now loaded in the preview.
messages.savedDescription: The draft is saved in this browser only. It has not been sent to the backend.

preview.copy: This preview shows package defaults and local draft changes on a dense admin surface before they are written into a source theme package.
preview.description: Preview the current token draft on admin-style controls and modules.
preview.heading: Token-driven admin surface
preview.title: Live preview

package.title: Active package
package.description: The current build reads this package manifest, token map, local assets, recipes, and template exports.

recipes.title: Component recipes
recipes.description: Recipes document which theme tokens control reusable Aoi primitives and patterns.

release.description: Production changes happen by editing a source theme package, regenerating tokens, and rebuilding the WebUI. Runtime backend publishing stays disabled.
release.sourceOnly: No backend theme release is pending. Copy the example package, change the active theme ID, regenerate, and rebuild to publish a custom skin.
release.title: Release boundary

templates.title: Template exports
templates.description: Theme packages can replace shell and page templates through typed React exports while business pages keep shared API, i18n, auth, and primitive contracts.

parameters.title: System parameters
parameters.description: Manage backend-backed system parameter records through /api/v1/system/parameters.

filters.description: These fields map directly to the backend parameter list endpoint.
filters.name: Name
filters.title: Filters

form.description: Fields map directly to the backend create and update request bodies.
form.name: Name

delete.bulkDescription: This will delete {{count}} selected parameter record(s) through the backend bulk delete endpoint.
delete.singleDescription: This will delete {{name}} through the backend parameter delete endpoint.

messages.requiredDescription: Name, key, and value are required before saving.

states.permissionDescription: The current account needs the parameter:read permission to view system parameters.
states.storageUnavailableDescription: The backend reports that system_parameters is not persisted yet. Run the required migrations before using parameter workflows.

organizations.title: Organizations
organizations.description: Manage tenant context through backend-supported organization listing, creation, current organization update, and organization switching endpoints.

filters.description: These fields map directly to the backend organization list query while preserving service-side sorting and pagination semantics.
filters.title: Filters

list.description: {{total}} organizations match the current filters, with {{count}} shown on this page.
list.title: Organization list

current.description: The backend currently only allows updating the current organization name. Code and status remain read-only.
current.successDescription: {{name}} was saved as the current organization name.
current.title: Current organization

create.description: Create a new organization with a unique code and name. The backend assigns the creator as owner.
create.successDescription: {{name}} was created through the backend organization endpoint.
create.title: Create organization

switch.successDescription: Switched to {{name}} and refreshed the current session identity.

states.missingOrgDescription: The current session token does not include an orgId claim, so organization management endpoints cannot be called.
states.permissionDescription: The current account needs org:read permission to view organizations, plus org:create or org:update for changes.
states.storageUnavailableDescription: The backend reports that IAM organization storage is not persisted yet. Run the required migrations before using organization workflows.

validation.code: Organization code must start with a lowercase letter and contain only lowercase letters, numbers, underscores, or hyphens, up to 64 characters.
validation.name: Enter an organization name.
validation.title: Organization details incomplete

users.title: Users
users.description: Read and manage organization members through backend-supported /api/v1/orgs/:orgId/users and invitation endpoints.

filters.description: These fields map directly to the backend user list endpoint and keep the current organization scope.
filters.title: Filters

member.roleSuccessDescription: {{name}} now uses the selected organization role.

invite.debugDeliveryDescription: The backend returned local debug delivery values because no external mail delivery was used.
invite.debugSuccessDescription: Invitation for {{email}} was created. Use the debug token or URL below to accept it locally.
invite.description: Create an invitation with an email address and an existing organization role.
invite.successDescription: Invitation for {{email}} was created through the backend invitation endpoint.
invite.title: Invite user
invite.validationDescription: Enter a valid email address and choose an available role before sending the invitation.

invitation.confirmRevokeDescription: This will revoke the pending invitation for {{email}}. The backend will reject future acceptance with that token.
invitation.title: Invitations

states.missingOrgDescription: The current session token does not include an orgId claim, so organization user endpoints cannot be called.
states.permissionDescription: The current account needs user:read permission to view users and user:invite or user:update for invitation and member changes.
states.storageUnavailableDescription: The backend reports that IAM user storage is not persisted yet. Run the required migrations before using user workflows.

roles.title: Roles
roles.description: Read, create, and update organization roles through backend-supported IAM role and permission endpoints. System roles stay read-only.

fields.descriptionHelp: The backend trims the description and stores it with the role.
fields.name: Role name

create.description: Create a custom role and write its initial permission set through the backend role endpoint.
create.successDescription: {{name}} was created and the role list is refreshing.
create.title: Create custom role

edit.description: Select a custom role, then update its display name, description, and permission set. System roles are excluded because the backend rejects updates to them.
edit.descriptionHelp: Permission updates use replacement semantics, so the checked set becomes the role's full permission set.
edit.noSelectionDescription: Choose an editable custom role before saving.
edit.noneDescription: The backend only returned system roles. Create a custom role before using the editor.
edit.successDescription: {{name}} was updated through the backend role endpoint.
edit.title: Edit custom role

permissions.createDescription: Choose existing permission codes from /api/v1/orgs/:orgId/permissions. Invalid backend permission codes are not invented in the UI.
permissions.editDescription: The selected permission codes replace the current custom role policy set.
permissions.emptyDescription: No permissions match the current search or object filter.

validation.code: Use a lowercase role code that starts with a letter and only contains letters, numbers, underscores, or hyphens.
validation.name: Enter a role name before submitting.
validation.permissions: Use non-empty permission codes returned by the backend permission catalog.
validation.title: Role details incomplete

states.loadingDescription: Reading protected IAM roles and permissions.
states.missingOrgDescription: The current session token does not include an orgId claim, so organization role endpoints cannot be called.
states.permissionDescription: The current account needs role:read and permission:read to view roles, plus role:create or role:update for changes.

sessions.title: Sessions
sessions.description: Read organization session records from /api/v1/orgs/:orgId/sessions, show product and platform dimensions, and call the backend-supported session revocation endpoint.

filters.description: These fields map directly to the backend session list endpoint. Organization scope lists visible org sessions; self scope lists the current user unless a user ID is supplied.
filters.title: Filters

revoke.confirmDescription: This will revoke session {{id}} for user {{userId}} from IP {{ip}}. The backend will reject future requests from that session.
revoke.currentSessionTitle: Current session cannot be revoked here
revoke.currentSessionDescription: The current access token belongs to session {{id}}. Revoke it from a different session.
revoke.successDescription: Session {{id}} was handled by the backend revocation endpoint. The list is refreshing.

states.missingOrgDescription: The current session token does not include an orgId claim, so organization-scoped session endpoints cannot be called.
states.permissionDescription: The current account needs session:read permission to view sessions.
states.storageUnavailableDescription: The backend reports that sessions are not persisted yet. Run the required migrations before using session workflows.

security.title: Security center
security.description: Review the current account, organization, session, and platform dimension, then enable or rotate a TOTP factor through the backend MFA endpoints.

fields.mfaCodeHelp: Enter the 6-digit code shown by your authenticator app.

mfa.disabledDescription: Generate a backend TOTP secret, add it to your authenticator app, and submit the current code.
mfa.enabledDescription: This account requires an MFA code at login. Complete verification immediately after rotating the secret.
mfa.setupEmptyDescription: After generation, the backend returns a one-time secret and otpauth URL for this page only.
mfa.title: MFA

messages.copyDeniedDescription: The browser did not allow writing {{label}}. Select and copy it manually.
messages.enabledDescription: MFA was verified by the backend and the page secret has been cleared.
messages.secretGeneratedDescription: The secret is shown only in page memory. Add it to your authenticator app now.

states.identityLoadingDescription: Reading the current account, organization, and session context from the backend.

apiTokens.title: API tokens
apiTokens.description: Read, issue, and revoke organization API tokens through the backend-supported /api/v1/orgs/:orgId/api-tokens endpoints.

filters.description: These fields map directly to the backend API token list endpoint.
filters.title: Filters

list.description: {{count}} API tokens match the current filters.
list.title: Token list

issue.description: Choose an organization member and one of that member's assigned roles. The backend returns the full secret only once after creation.
issue.noAssignableRoleDescription: The selected member does not have a role returned by the backend role catalog.
issue.remarkHelp: Optional note stored with this token.
issue.successDescription: Token {{id}} was created by the backend. Copy the secret before closing this panel.
issue.title: Issue API token
issue.validationDescription: Select a user, select an assigned role, and choose a supported validity period.

issued.description: The full token is kept only in page memory for this response. It is not saved to the session store.
issued.title: Copy the new API token
issued.warningDescription: Copy this token now. The backend stores only the hashed token and cannot return the full value again.

copy.exampleDeniedDescription: The browser did not allow copying the example request.
copy.tokenDeniedDescription: The browser did not allow copying the token.

revoke.confirmDescription: This will revoke token {{id}} for {{user}} with prefix {{prefix}}. Future requests using that token will be rejected by the backend.
revoke.successDescription: Token {{id}} was handled by the backend revocation endpoint. The list is refreshing.

states.loadingDescription: Reading protected IAM API token records.
states.missingOrgDescription: The current session token does not include an orgId claim, so organization-scoped API token endpoints cannot be called.
states.permissionDescription: The current account needs API token permissions for this workflow.
states.storageUnavailableDescription: The backend reports that API tokens are not persisted yet. Run the required migrations before using token workflows.

auditLogs.title: Audit logs
auditLogs.description: Read IAM audit records from /api/v1/orgs/:orgId/audit-logs with product and platform dimensions plus backend-supported action, user, time, cursor, and limit filters.

filters.description: These fields map directly to the backend audit-log endpoint. Time filters are sent as RFC3339 values; the cursor reads records older than the given log ID.
filters.title: Filters

list.description: Showing {{count}} audit logs from a backend limit of {{limit}}.
list.title: Audit log list

states.missingOrgDescription: The current session token does not include an orgId claim, so organization-scoped audit endpoints cannot be called.
states.permissionDescription: The current account needs audit:read permission to view IAM audit records.

loginLogs.title: Login logs
loginLogs.description: Read auth.login audit records from /api/v1/orgs/:orgId/audit-logs and show product and platform dimensions. User, time, and limit filters stay backend-supported; IP is filtered locally from the returned page.

filters.description: Login logs always send action=auth.login. User, from, to, and limit are sent to the backend; IP address uses the old admin behavior and filters the current result locally.
filters.title: Filters

list.description: Showing {{count}} {{action}} login logs from a backend limit of {{limit}}.
list.title: Login log list

states.loadingDescription: Reading protected IAM login audit records.
states.missingOrgDescription: The current session token does not include an orgId claim, so organization-scoped login log endpoints cannot be called.
states.permissionDescription: The current account needs audit:read permission to view login logs.

operationRecords.title: Operation records
operationRecords.description: Read HTTP operation records from /api/v1/system/operation-records with backend-supported filters, pagination, request summaries, and response summaries.

filters.description: These fields map directly to the backend operation record endpoint. Exact status takes precedence over status class.
filters.title: Filters

delete.bulkDescription: This will delete {{count}} selected operation record(s) through the backend bulk delete endpoint.

states.permissionDescription: The current account needs the operation-record:read permission to view operation records.
states.deletePermissionDescription: The current account needs the operation:delete permission to delete operation records.
states.storageUnavailableDescription: The backend reports that operation records are not persisted yet. Run the required migrations before using operation record workflows.

errorLogs.title: Error logs
errorLogs.description: Read HTTP error operation records from /api/v1/system/operation-records. The default view is 5xx and only exposes backend-supported status class, status, method, path, and pagination filters.

filters.description: Error logs send statusClass=5xx by default; choosing 4xx or all errors changes that status class. Exact status takes precedence and is sent without status class.
filters.title: Filters

list.description: {{count}} error records match the current filters.
list.title: Error record list

states.permissionDescription: The current account needs operation-record:read permission to view error logs.
states.storageUnavailableDescription: The backend reports that operation records are not persisted yet. Run the required migrations before using error log workflows.

plugins.title: Plugins
plugins.description: Read remote plugin host snapshots, health, and capability declarations from the backend plugin management contract.

filters.description: These filters run on the current backend plugin snapshot response.
filters.title: Filters

list.description: {{count}} of {{total}} plugin instances match the current filters.
list.title: Plugin instances

detail.description: The detail endpoint returns one snapshot for the selected plugin ID, preferring an online instance when multiple instances exist.
detail.name: Name
detail.title: Plugin detail

health.description: Health is read from the plugin host and may return offline when the selected plugin has no online instance.
health.title: Health

capabilities.description: Capabilities are declared by the remote plugin and are shown as metadata only; the frontend does not invoke them.
capabilities.title: Capabilities

states.capabilitiesEmptyDescription: The selected plugin snapshot did not return capability declarations.
states.disabledDescription: The backend reports that plugin hosting or plugin management is disabled.
states.emptyDescription: No remote plugins are currently registered.
states.healthEmptyDescription: No health snapshot was returned for the selected plugin.
states.healthLoadingDescription: Reading plugin health from the backend host.
states.noMatchesDescription: No plugin instances match the current filters.
states.offlineDescription: The backend reports that the selected plugin is offline.
states.permissionDescription: The current account needs plugin:read permission to view plugin management data.

versions.title: Versions
versions.description: Read system release package records from /api/v1/system/versions with backend-supported filters.

filters.description: Only filters supported by the backend list endpoint are available here.
filters.title: Filters

states.permissionDescription: The current account needs the version:read permission to view versions.
states.storageUnavailableDescription: The backend reports that system_versions is not persisted yet. Run the required migrations before using version package workflows.
states.detailLoadingDescription: Reading the selected version package from the backend.
states.dictionarySourceUnavailableDescription: The backend reports dictionary storage is unavailable, so dictionary sources cannot be selected for export.
states.sourceLoadingDescription: Reading backend menu, API, and dictionary sources for package export.

delete.bulkDescription: Delete {{count}} selected version records. This removes the stored package records through the backend contract.
delete.singleDescription: Delete {{name}}. This removes the stored package record through the backend contract.

detail.description: Read the backend-returned package snapshot for this version record.
detail.title: Version detail

export.description: Create a backend-managed release package from selected menus, API routes, and persisted dictionaries.
export.title: Create release package

import.description: Paste or choose a JSON package generated by the backend version export contract.
import.invalidDescription: The package must be valid JSON and include version.name and version.code.
import.title: Import version package

messages.exportedDescription: {{name}} was created from backend version sources.

trafficHijack.title: Traffic hijack monitoring
trafficHijack.description: Use backend HTTP(S) probes to detect DNS, TLS, redirect, status code, content fingerprint, and latency anomalies.

events.description: Incidents are grouped by target, reason, and evidence, then closed when the route recovers.
events.empty: No events match the current filters.
events.title: In-app events

form.description: After saving, the backend scheduler probes the target at its interval. Private targets are blocked by default.
form.title: Target configuration

results.chartDescription: Shows total probe latency and status code changes for recent results.
results.description: Recent probe results are retained in a short window to locate the failed stage and evidence.
results.emptyDescription: After a target is saved and probed, real sample data appears here.
results.title: Recent results

targets.description: Targets support HTTP(S) only, send no body, and follow up to 5 redirects.
targets.title: Probe targets

notFound.title: Platform console page not found
notFound.description: This platform console route does not exist, or the current backend has not exposed the matching contract.

mediaResumable.title: Resumable upload
mediaResumable.description: Check sessions, upload chunks, complete merges, or abort sessions through the media resumable endpoints already exposed by the Go backend.

a11y.fileInput: Choose a file for resumable upload

upload.title: Upload session
upload.description: Choose a category first, then choose a file. The file hash is computed in the browser and sent to the backend for session checking without persistent local storage.
upload.emptyFileDescription: Choose a file to compute SHA-256 and ask the backend whether a resumable session already exists.

fields.categoryHelp: The category is submitted when the session is checked. Reset before changing it after a file is selected.

result.title: Upload result
result.description: {{name}} has been merged into a media asset by the backend.

messages.emptyFileDescription: Choose a file larger than 0 bytes.
messages.fileTooLargeDescription: The selected file is {{size}}, while the backend upload limit is {{limit}}.
messages.hashUnsupportedDescription: Resumable upload requires Web Crypto SHA-256 support. Use a browser with Web Crypto enabled.
messages.readyDescription: The backend returned {{count}} missing chunks. You can start uploading.
messages.resumeDescription: The backend already has {{count}} uploaded chunks. Upload will continue from the missing chunks.
messages.duplicateDescription: The backend returned a completed asset, so another upload is not needed.
messages.uploadCompletedDescription: {{name}} finished resumable upload and was written as a media asset.
messages.abortCompletedDescription: Session {{sessionId}} was aborted, and temporary chunks are cleaned by the backend.
messages.storageUnavailableDescription: The backend reports object storage is unavailable, so new upload sessions or chunks cannot be written right now.

identifier.label: Email or username
identifier.help: Use an identifier supported by the backend.

username.label: Username
username.help: Used for admin display and login.

orgCode.label: Organization code
orgCode.help: Only uses the backend-supported organization signup field.

captchaCode.label: Captcha
captchaCode.help: Use the challenge shown by the backend.

mfaCode.label: MFA code
mfaCode.help: Use the one-time code from your authenticator app.

newPassword.label: New password
newPassword.help: The backend validates and stores the new password securely.

resetToken.label: Reset token
resetToken.help: Use the token from the reset notification or debug delivery.

api.requestFailed: The request failed. Please try again later.
api.unauthorized: Your session has expired. Please log in again.

route.title: Page unavailable
route.description: This path could not render. Go back home and try again.
route.unexpectedDescription: The frontend hit an unexpected rendering error.

validation.password: Password must be at least 8 characters.

notFound.title: Page not found
notFound.description: Check the address or return home.

home.title: Aoi Admin: Full-Stack Product Platform Base
home.description: Aoi Admin reuses account, access, organization, config, audit, plugin, media, and version capabilities for a core platform and multiple product lines.
home.ogDescription: One Go backend and React frontend for the public site, first-install setup, platform console, and future product-line entries.

about.title: Aoi Admin Platform Architecture and Positioning
about.description: Learn how Aoi Admin uses a shared core platform for accounts, access, organizations, config, audit, and future product-line expansion.
about.ogTitle: Aoi Admin core platform and product-line architecture
about.ogDescription: The core platform owns common capabilities while independent product lines extend their own sites, consoles, and domain modules.

blog.title: Aoi Admin Product Platform Notes
blog.description: Read Aoi Admin platform positioning, product-line architecture, Markdown content system updates, i18n decisions, and engineering logs.
blog.ogDescription: Engineering decisions for the Aoi Admin shared product base, React unified frontend, setup wizard, and platform console.

login.title: Log in to Aoi
login.description: Log in to the Aoi Admin platform console through the backend-supported authentication endpoint.
login.ogDescription: Access the Aoi Admin platform console with the shared React auth entry point.

signup.title: Sign up for Aoi
signup.description: Create an Aoi Admin platform account using the backend-supported signup contract.
signup.ogDescription: Create an account for the Aoi Admin platform console through the shared auth flow.

signupVerify.title: Verify Aoi email
signupVerify.description: Confirm an Aoi Admin email verification token and activate the pending account.
signupVerify.ogDescription: Complete the email verification step for the shared React auth flow.

passwordForgot.title: Recover Aoi password
passwordForgot.description: Request a backend-supported one-time password reset token for an Aoi account.
passwordForgot.ogDescription: Start password recovery through the shared React auth entry point.

passwordReset.title: Reset Aoi password
passwordReset.description: Reset an Aoi account password with a backend-issued one-time token.
passwordReset.ogDescription: Use a one-time token to reset an Aoi account password.

invitation.title: Accept Aoi invitation
invitation.description: Accept an organization invitation and prepare an account for the Aoi Admin platform console.
invitation.ogDescription: Join an invited organization through the shared React auth flow.

terms.title: Aoi Terms of Service
terms.description: Review the draft Aoi Admin platform terms structure before final launch copy is approved.
terms.ogDescription: Draft terms structure for the Aoi Admin public site, setup flow, and platform console.

privacy.title: Aoi Privacy Policy
privacy.description: Review the draft Aoi privacy structure for auth, setup, locale preference, and backend API interactions.
privacy.ogDescription: Draft privacy structure for the Aoi Admin React frontend and Go backend integrations.
